summary: Premise-verified Session 82 continuation audit using the founder-requested infrastructure rubric plus game-loop and public-release lenses. The prior six-item plan remains shipped. Four new local findings survive live-code verification: the visible Season Pass still resets on restart and mints claimed-looking rewards without durable entitlements; three experiment families remain embedded in the 4,027-line Worker composition root; gameplay tuning is duplicated between prose and private class constants without a release-bound balance proof; and npm reports a direct protobufjs denial-of-service advisory with a safe patch available while CI ignores moderate findings. External staging, email delivery, native Obelisk, three-human Alpha, revenue, and founder approval remain explicitly unobserved and cannot be fabricated.

sample.method: live code and contract survey; sample-codebase helper absent

gameLoop.boundary: The scheduled public vault-to-convoy loop, certified contracts, loop evidence, and Prediction League are now reachable and coherent. Retention remains materially weakened by a process-local Season Pass whose claims have no durable entitlement effect. No three-human Alpha corpus exists, so qualitative balance changes remain out of scope; this audit proves the current envelope instead of inventing tuning evidence.

passing: branding, legal, and AGPL exception attribution
documented immutable promotion and rollback contract
red: no staging or production GitHub environments or promotion evidence
no live staging smoke, digest parity, headers, Core Web Vitals, or theme observation
project-domain contact route has no DNS or Brevo delivery receipt
native Obelisk relying-party wiring is absent
three-human Alpha, revenue, rollback-drill, and founder-approval receipts are absent

topRisks: the visible Season Pass is a second, process-local progression truth beside the certified contract ledger
Worker.ts remains a 4,027-line route composition root with three near-identical experiment families embedded
gameplay tuning is duplicated between documentation and private constants without a digestible balance contract
a direct protobufjs moderate denial-of-service advisory is currently below the CI audit threshold
startup brief rendering mixes broad parsing, healing, status mutation, and telemetry
human-action parsing exists in three incompatible implementations

evidenceBoundary: Local automation cannot manufacture staging, project-domain delivery, native relying-party registration, distinct-human Alpha, revenue, rollback observation, or founder approval.
The registry still classifies VaultFront as app while local project truth says game. CANON-018 forbids editing the sibling registry; correction must travel through signed Ark cargo.
No dependency install, paid service, license change, or sibling tree edit is required for these local items.
No AI feature is proposed: the verified gaps are core-loop reachability, certified progression, analytics truth, and infrastructure composition.

rejectedPremises.evidence: Live scripts already import and use scripts/lib/write-project-status.mjs; the prior audit premise is stale.

rejectedPremises.candidate: Tune convoy timings from code review
rejectedPremises.evidence: The core public loop is currently disabled and no three-human Alpha corpus exists. Restore reachability and certified evidence before tuning.

rejectedPremises.candidate: Treat the current health route regex as staging health
rejectedPremises.evidence: A source declaration cannot prove a reachable or healthy runtime; the evidence model must separate contract from observation.

rejectedPremises.candidate: Delete old deploy and migration workflows by age
rejectedPremises.evidence: The workflows remain referenced by architecture and runbook surfaces; age alone is not orphan evidence.
A bulk upgrade remains unjustified. Live npm audit identifies one bounded direct protobufjs patch, which is promoted as a separate package-trust-gated item.

rejectedPremises.candidate: Promote local theme proof or generated release evidence into launch proof
rejectedPremises.evidence: Local and static evidence explicitly cannot prove an external origin, live headers, Core Web Vitals, email delivery, identity, human use, or revenue.

rejectedPremises.candidate: Configure Brevo, Cloudflare, or Obelisk merely because credentials are READY
rejectedPremises.evidence: Secrets discovery reports all three capabilities READY, but the repository has no approved external staging origin, Brevo inbound webhook destination, or Obelisk relying-party callback contract. Creating provider state without those scoped targets would fabricate readiness rather than complete it.

rejectedPremises.candidate: Tune reward values from code review
rejectedPremises.evidence: The documented reward envelope lacks three-human playtest evidence. The valid local action is to make the current envelope executable and digest-bound, not to change balance constants from intuition.

items.axis: feature depth / core loop / ux / soul fidelity
items.title: Make every scheduled public playlist actually run the VaultFront loop
items.why: MapPlaylist returns FFA, team, special, and ranked public configs without vaultSitesEnabled or intelOperationsEnabled. Both default false, and GameRunner installs VaultFrontExecution only when either is true. Private and single-player paths explicitly enable them, proving the public omission is real.
items.coreLoopStep: public matchmaking → vault capture → convoy decision → interception/delivery → certified progression
items.recipe: Centralize a public VaultFront config policy, apply it to FFA/team/special/1v1 configurations, and add integration-grade contract tests proving each scheduled public config installs the VaultFront execution path without changing private-host opt-in semantics.

L1.recipe: Set both feature flags on public configs.

L2.recipe: Centralize the policy and add MapPlaylist coverage for FFA, team, special, and 1v1.

L3.recipe: Add a GameRunner reachability ratchet proving every scheduled public path installs VaultFrontExecution while private custom configuration remains explicit.

executionLog: L3 shipped: centralized public feature policy covers FFA, team, special, and ranked paths; GameRunner predicate and five integration assertions preserve private opt-in semantics.

items.title: Replace client-authoritative seasonal counters with a certified durable contract ledger
items.why: WinModal computes arbitrary deltas and POSTs them to an endpoint whose schema has no game identity, upper bounds, or replay protection. Worker adds those values to a process-local Map, allowing inflation and restart loss despite the project's certified progression posture.
items.coreLoopStep: certified match result → exactly-once seasonal contract progress → durable next-match prescription
items.recipe: Create a typed CertifiedSeasonContractStore with PostgreSQL durability and an honest process-local fallback; derive bounded contract metrics only from AuthoritativeMatchOutcome; key events by player, season, and game for idempotency; make the client GET-only; return durability and evidence metadata; add duplicate, restart, transaction, and authorization tests.

L1.recipe: Disable client mutation and update contracts from MatchProgression in memory.

L2.recipe: Add typed certified match derivation, idempotency, read-only client refresh, and focused tests.

L3.recipe: Add PostgreSQL event/progress tables, transaction-bound replay safety, explicit durability/evidence receipts, route extraction, and restart tests.

executionLog: L3 shipped: certified match derivation, PostgreSQL event/progress transaction, per-game replay safety, honest process-local fallback, GET-only client refresh, route extraction, and authorization/adversarial tests.

items.title: Measure the vault-to-convoy loop from certified game transitions instead of browser timestamps
items.why: WinModal reconstructs early/mid/late phases from an unbound sessionStorage stream and Date.now minus match length. The stream is not game-scoped or cleared and counts UI commands rather than first-vault latency, convoy completion, interception, repeat cycles, or abandonment.
items.coreLoopStep: authoritative transitions → per-game loop evidence → truthful cohort summary → human playtest tuning
items.recipe: Carry GameServer's authoritative intent funnel plus certified VaultFront result counters into a game-bound LoopEvidence receipt, persist one idempotent record per game and actor where available, derive first-cycle and repeat-cycle metrics without client claims, expose a privacy-minimal summary, retire the client funnel POST, and test cross-match isolation and duplicate completion.

L1.recipe: Bind existing client telemetry to gameId and clear it at match boundaries.

L2.recipe: Build a server-certified per-game receipt from GameServer intentFunnel and result counters, with a read-only summary.

L3.recipe: Add durable idempotent storage, privacy-minimal cohort derivation, route extraction, and adversarial cross-match/replay tests.

executionLog: L3 shipped: first-vault and first-outcome ticks plus server intent funnel persist as one privacy-minimal aggregate per game; browser/sessionStorage mutation is retired and admin summary fails closed.

items.title: Separate the static health-route contract from a live runtime health observation
items.why: generate-release-evidence regex-matches source for app.get('/_health'), stamps a fresh observedAt, and records status pass under healthEndpoint. That proves declaration, not reachability or a healthy deployment.
items.recipe: Rename the static evidence dimension to healthRouteContract, model live healthObservation as a provenance-bearing external receipt, keep runtimeAdvertised false without it, update manifest and readiness consumers, and add an adversarial test showing a declared-but-unobserved or 503 endpoint cannot become release-ready.

L1.recipe: Rename the static field and copy to remove the false endpoint implication.

L2.recipe: Add separate typed contract and observation dimensions plus generator tests.

L3.recipe: Add signed/provenance-ready observation ingestion, readiness invariants, and declared-but-unreachable/503 adversarial fixtures.

executionLog: L3 shipped: source declaration is healthRouteContract; runtimeAdvertised requires fresh provenance-bearing HTTP 200 healthObservation with digest; unobserved and 503 fixtures remain non-ready.

items.title: Turn Worker.ts into a composition root with bounded domain routers
items.why: Worker.ts is roughly 4,000 physical lines and mixes identity, policy, readiness, experiments, AI, game lifecycle, clans, tournaments, progression, replay, and telemetry. This makes every route change a high-blast-radius edit and leaves many behaviors testable only through source scanning.
items.recipe: Extract the season-contract, loop-evidence, and spectator-prediction domains behind injected route dependencies; preserve RoutePolicy bindings and authentication at the seam; add direct router tests; and introduce a production-code composition budget ratchet so future domains cannot silently re-accumulate in Worker.ts.

L1.recipe: Extract one new domain router with injected dependencies.

L2.recipe: Extract the three changed domains and add direct auth/error route tests.

L3.recipe: Add shared composition types, policy-binding tests, a Worker size/domain ratchet, and source-scan replacement for extracted routes.

executionLog: L3 shipped: season, loop-evidence, and prediction routers use injected seams with direct tests; route inventory scans extracted routers; Worker has a 4,080-line ratchet and extracted-domain reclamation checks.

items.axis: new feature / spectator engagement / retention / security
items.title: Complete the Prediction League as a reachable, restart-durable spectator loop
items.why: The leaderboard UI is reachable, but no client calls prediction-league/predict; a separate narrator crowd route accepts votes without adding them to league history. PredictionLeagueStore calls itself persistent while storing pending and history exclusively in Maps, so restarts erase the league.
items.coreLoopStep: spectate → commit one authenticated prediction → certified match resolution → durable accuracy/rank → return
items.recipe: Unify crowd vote and league submission behind one authenticated prediction contract, persist pending predictions and resolved history with game/player uniqueness, resolve only from MatchProgression's certified outcome, add a compact spectator prediction surface with live aggregate feedback, and test replay, restart, privacy, and empty-state behavior.

L1.recipe: Wire an authenticated client prediction action to the existing league route.

L2.recipe: Unify vote/league routes, add idempotent memory behavior, live aggregate UI, and router tests.

L3.recipe: Add PostgreSQL persistence, certified resolution receipts, restart/replay tests, and privacy-minimal leaderboard identities.

executionLog: L3 shipped: spectator-only accessible pick panel, authenticated actor binding, PostgreSQL pending/history ledger, advisory-lock submit/resolve serialization, certified outcome resolution, private stats, and replay/restart truth tests.

items.title: Make the visible Season Pass a certified durable entitlement ledger
items.why: SeasonMilestoneStore is a process-local Map updated by the certified progression spine without game identifiers. Restarting erases progress and claims, replaying an outcome can double-count activity, and claim() only flips an in-memory boolean while the UI presents a title, badge, or gold-looking reward as earned. This is a second progression authority beside the durable certified contract ledger.
items.coreLoopStep: certified match → exactly-once milestone progress → durable entitlement claim → visible profile identity
items.recipe: Replace activity-by-activity mutation with one certified game-bound Season Pass update per player; persist progress events, aggregate progress, and claims in PostgreSQL with honest process-local fallback; bind claims to the authenticated actor; represent rewards as durable title/badge entitlements rather than ungranted currency; expose durability/evidence metadata; extract the routes; and add restart, replay, authorization, and entitlement tests.

L1.recipe: Key in-memory activity by game and stop presenting ungranted rewards.

L2.recipe: Create a typed certified store, actor-bound routes, idempotent game updates, and direct route/store tests.

L3.recipe: Add PostgreSQL event/progress/claim transactions, restart-safe entitlements, explicit durability receipts, route extraction, and adversarial replay/auth tests.

executionLog: Session 82: replaced the process-local activity Map with a certified player/season/game event ledger, aggregate progress table, and cosmetic entitlement table; production fails closed when configured PostgreSQL is unavailable.

The progression spine now emits one game-bound receipt per player, replay is rejected by a composite primary key, and claims transact against locked progress before materializing title/badge entitlements that restore after restart.

Season routes moved behind an actor-bound injected router, both reads and writes reject cross-player access, the UI exposes postgres versus process-local durability, and focused replay/auth/restart/transaction/schema tests plus TypeScript and contract verification pass.

items.axis: feedback loop / observability / game depth / automation
items.title: Turn VaultFront balance into a self-verifying release-bound envelope
items.why: GAMEPLAY_DESIGN declares itself authoritative, but the reward and cadence constants remain private fields spread through VaultFrontExecution and manually duplicated in prose. Property tests use Math.random and prove only a few relations. A plausible doc/code drift can therefore ship without a deterministic artifact showing fairness bounds, monotonicity, caps, mutator effects, or the exact tuning digest used by a release.
items.coreLoopStep: shared tuning authority → exhaustive deterministic scenarios → signed balance receipt → release lineage
items.recipe: Extract the core convoy/cadence tuning into one typed frozen authority consumed by execution; expose a pure reward planner; generate a deterministic balance-envelope receipt across boundary and seeded scenario grids; fail on documented monotonicity, cap, comeback, and mutator invariants; bind the receipt digest into release evidence and public agent capability metadata; and replace random property checks with reproducible counterexamples.

L1.recipe: Export core tuning constants and assert a few documented values.

L2.recipe: Create a shared pure reward planner and deterministic grid tests with counterexample output.

L3.recipe: Generate a digestible balance envelope, self-verify it, attach it to release lineage and dual-audience surfaces, and ratchet the build against drift.

executionLog: Session 82: created one versioned convoy tuning authority and one pure reward planner consumed by DefaultConfig, live VaultFrontExecution, deterministic tests, and the envelope generator.

The build now exhaustively verifies 28,125 boundary scenarios across multiplier clamps, reward floors, distance/risk monotonicity, reduced-reward ordering, and combo ordering; it emits zero counterexamples and stable tuning/scenario digests.

Production builds gate on the envelope, release evidence binds artifact and source digests into a self-validating lineage node, agents.json advertises only a read-only evidence endpoint, and the production build plus focused gameplay/release/public-surface tests pass.

items.title: Extract the experiment control plane from Worker.ts behind one bounded router
items.why: Worker.ts is 4,027 lines against a 4,040-line ceiling and still embeds dock, recap, runtime, and unified experiment routes with repeated identity, integrity, admin, and summary logic. The prior router extraction created the seam but left this high-churn control plane one small edit away from breaking the composition budget.
items.recipe: Move all experiment assignment/event/summary/admin routes into an injected ExperimentRouter; preserve policy binding and actor/admin checks; test invalid variants, replayed event IDs, summaries, and unified results directly; teach route inventory and the composition ratchet about the extracted domain; and lower the Worker budget to bank the reduction.

L1.recipe: Extract the three event POST routes.

L2.recipe: Extract all assignment/event/summary routes with injected dependencies and focused tests.

L3.recipe: Extract the unified admin surface, add policy/integrity adversarial tests, scan the router in inventory, and ratchet Worker below its new measured size.

executionLog: Session 82: extracted twelve assignment, event, summary, admin, and outcome routes into ExperimentRouter with an injected control plane while preserving the legacy hash algorithm and objective-event vocabulary.
Worker.ts contracted from 4,029 to 3,138 physical lines; the executable ceiling is now 3,160 and route reclamation is rejected for the fourth extracted domain.

Direct tests pin deterministic assignments, reject duplicate and forged variants, verify derived outcome math, and inventory all routes/policy bindings; TypeScript, focused tests, and verify:contracts pass.

items.title: Patch the direct protobuf parser denial-of-service advisory and ratchet CI
items.why: Live npm audit reports direct dependency protobufjs 7.6.3 inside the affected 7.5.0–7.6.4 range for GHSA-j3f2-48v5-ccww. A non-major 7.6.5 fix is available, but CI runs npm audit at high severity and therefore permits this known moderate availability risk indefinitely.
items.recipe: Run Obelisk package-trust for protobufjs@7.6.5, update only the pinned dependency and lockfile, verify the production build and protocol imports, change CI to audit at moderate severity, and add a contract test that prevents the threshold from drifting upward.

L1.recipe: Apply the safe patch version and regenerate the lockfile.

L2.recipe: Verify build/tests and npm audit returns zero known vulnerabilities.

L3.recipe: Package-trust the patch, ratchet CI at moderate severity, and add a workflow-contract test.

executionLog: L3 shipped: Obelisk REVIEW was resolved with live registry evidence (13-year package lineage, stable maintainers, BSD-3-Clause, official source, exact integrity, 19-day cooldown); protobufjs is pinned at 7.6.5 with lifecycle scripts disabled during install, npm audit reports zero vulnerabilities, CI now rejects moderate advisories, the workflow/lock integrity test passes, and production build succeeds.
